middleware.description: Refuses actions that require an active-status Athlete row. Applied to routes that touch an Athlete whose status must be `active` (creating an enrollment against an archived athlete is refused). Resolves the target Athlete from the route parameter (`{athlete}`) and returns 422 `ATHLETE_NOT_ACTIVE` when status != 'active'. Distinct from the observer refusal — the middleware fails fast at the route boundary so the caller doesn't burn a request through the full pipeline for a known-bad state.
auto_applied_to: /athletes/{athlete}/consents (POST — cannot record consent for archived athlete)
/athletes/{athlete}/link-user (POST — cannot link user to archived athlete)

middleware.description: Field-level permission enforcer for the medical sub-resource. Refuses the whole `/athletes/{athlete}/medical` endpoint (GET + PATCH) when: (a) the tenant lacks the `athlete_medical_tracking` entitlement, OR (b) the caller lacks `athletes.view.medical` (for GET) / `athletes.manage.medical` (for PATCH). This is separate from the field-level redaction that applies on the parent `/athletes/{athlete}` GET — the sub-resource is entirely permission-gated so the middleware can 402 / 403 the request before any DB fetch.

Same shape as athlete.enforce_medical_permission but for the emergency-contact sub-resource. Refuses when caller lacks `athletes.view.emergency_contact` (GET) / `athletes.manage.emergency_contact` (PATCH). Adult athletes can view/manage their own via `.own`.

consumed.note: The permission-based authorize middleware wraps the ability checks defined in policies.json + permissions.json. Route-level `permission` metadata resolves through this middleware.

ResolveScope narrows queries by the caller's active branch scope. Applied globally to authenticated tenant routes; athlete routes rely on it for the branch narrowing described in policies.json (a branch_manager sees their branch's athletes only).
